title: Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots reportedly sign WR Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million.
Doubs played for Green Bay Packers, recording 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns in 2025.
New England acquired Doubs following receiver Stefon Diggs' release, aiming to support QB Drake Maye.

title: Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Airbnb offers a $750 bonus to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston, for renting entire homes.
Searches for stays in 16 host cities, including Boston, increased by 80% compared to last year.
Boston hosts expect to earn $5,200 on average during the World Cup, second only to New York/New Jersey.

title: Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Rowan, 53, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Alzheimer's Association MA/NH Chapter.
Rowan, originally from Wellesley, Mass., is honoring his mother and grandmother who have both been affected by dementia.
The Boston Marathon has been a long-time inspiration for Rowan, deeply connected to his family's history in the area.

title: Fundraiser launched for Reddit tipster who helped identify suspect in Brown shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fundraiser was organized for a Reddit user who helped identify a suspect in a Brown University shooting.
The tipster, known as John, is living without permanent housing; more than $10,000 has been raised towards a $50,000 goal.
John's information led to the identification of Claudio Neves Valente, suspected in the Brown shooting and an MIT professor's death.

title: Joe Mazzulla praises Baylor Scheierman’s work ethic
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Joe Mazzulla praised Baylor Scheierman's work ethic and competitiveness after the Celtics beat the Cavaliers 109-98.
Scheierman recorded 16 points and 10 rebounds, hitting three 3-pointers in the second half against Cleveland.
Despite a thumb fracture, Scheierman continues to play significant minutes, contributing steadily for the Celtics this season.

title: Police are searching for North Shore suspect in string of indecent assaults
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Revere Police are searching for a suspect in multiple indecent assaults near the Beachmont MBTA Station.
The suspect is described as a Hispanic male, 5-foot-4 to 5-foot-7, with short dark hair and a thin build.
Three assaults occurred between January and March; police released video footage and urged the public to stay vigilant.

title: Wu announces millions in new funding for immigrant services
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu announced over $4.4 million in funding for immigrant services in Boston.
The funding comprises $3.1 million from philanthropic organizations and $1.3 million from the city.
Efforts include legal aid, mental health services, and a resource guide for immigrants.

title: Report: Flag football game featuring Tom Brady moved out of Saudi Arabia
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady's flag football game moved from Riyadh to Los Angeles due to Middle East tensions.
The Fanatics Flag Football Classic will occur on March 21 at LA's BMO Stadium, keeping the original date.
US and Israel's military actions against Iran influenced the relocation, with past NFL stars set to play.

title: Tell us: What’s been Joe Mazzulla’s best soundbite?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla has become known for his memorable soundbites during his tenure.
Boston.com seeks fans' input to determine Mazzulla's best soundbite for a sports history bracket.
Mazzulla's quotes range from tackling team expectations to sharing his love for the movie 'The Town.'

title: Why this Boston Marathon qualifier is running for the American Heart Association
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Blake Smith, 28, from Church Hill, Tennessee, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the American Heart Association.
Smith was diagnosed with Idiopathic Hypertrophic Subaortic Stenosis at 14 and vowed never to take running for granted.
He is part of the American Heart Association's fundraising team, raising money for a cause personal to him.
